encounter.reason: Cycle 3 chemo-immunotherapy administration; carboplatin dose reduction for cumulative toxicity; neuropathy management
encounter.service_provider: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

screening.name: ECOG performance status
screening.text: restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out light work

screening.name: CTCAE fatigue toxicity grade
screening.text: limiting instrumental activities of daily living

conditions.name: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe
conditions.text: known condition under active treatment; cough improvement noted as encouraging possible early tumor response

conditions.name: chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy
conditions.text: grade 1, bilateral fingertip numbness and tingling, no motor component; attributed to cumulative carboplatin toxicity

conditions.name: anemia
conditions.text: hemoglobin 10.6 g/dL, grade 1, multifactorial (chemotherapy myelosuppression, chronic disease); no transfusion indicated

conditions.name: essential hypertension
conditions.text: well controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily; BP 126/76 today

conditions.name: chemotherapy-related fatigue
conditions.text: fatigue worsened from grade 1 to grade 2, now limiting instrumental ADLs

medications.name: pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenous
medications.sig: 200 mg IV over 30 minutes every 3 weeks

medications.name: pemetrexed 500 mg/m² intravenous
medications.sig: 500 mg/m² IV over 10 minutes every 3 weeks

medications.name: carboplatin AUC 3.75 (338 mg) intravenous
medications.sig: AUC 3.75 (338 mg) IV over 30 minutes every 3 weeks
medications.text: dose reduced 25% from AUC 5 to AUC 3.75 (calculated 338 mg) due to cumulative toxicity (grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 peripheral neuropathy)

medications.name: gabapentin 300 mg oral tablet
medications.text: new medication started at this encounter for neuropathy management; pharmacist counseling provided by Dr. Pham

medications.name: dexamethasone 4 mg intravenous
medications.sig: 4 mg IV, given as pre-medication prior to chemotherapy infusion

medications.name: ondansetron 8 mg intravenous
medications.sig: 8 mg IV, given as pre-medication prior to chemotherapy infusion

medications.name: diphenhydramine 25 mg intravenous
medications.sig: 25 mg IV, given as pre-medication prior to chemotherapy infusion

document_references.description: Comprehensive infusion encounter note for Cycle 3 of pembrolizumab/carboplatin/pemetrexed chemo-immunotherapy. Documents pre-treatment assessment including subjective toxicity report (grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 neuropathy, grade 1 nausea, improved cough), pre-treatment labs and vitals, the decision to reduce carboplatin by 25% for cumulative toxicity, new diagnosis of chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy with gabapentin initiation, treatment administration details, and follow-up plan for Cycle 4 on March 10.

Documents administration of pre-medications (dexamethasone, ondansetron, diphenhydramine) and chemotherapy agents (pembrolizumab 200 mg IV, pemetrexed 500 mg/m² IV, carboplatin AUC 3.75/338 mg IV — dose reduced 25%), including infusion times, tolerability, and post-infusion monitoring.

document_references.type: adverse event report / clinical trial adverse event form
document_references.description: Documents new chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy (grade 1) and worsened fatigue (grade 2, increased from grade 1), carboplatin dose modification per protocol dose reduction algorithm. Reported to research coordinator per clinical trial protocol requirements.

diagnostic_reports.description: Pre-treatment CMP performed; creatinine 0.94, ALT 24, AST 26, albumin 3.0 reported individually. Full CMP panel components (glucose, BUN, sodium, potassium, chloride, CO2, calcium) are not enumerated in the narrative. Renal and hepatic function within acceptable limits for treatment per clinician assessment.
